key_highlights: Iran war and blockade push oil toward $120, gas to $4.30, creating stagflationary risks.
Fed holds rates steady amid Iran-fueled inflation; Powell stays on board until DOJ probe ends.
Big Tech earnings (Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet) beat expectations but AI spending surge raises capex concerns.
Yen intervention by Japan ($34.5B) fails to hold as energy prices counter; yen-oil correlation at 2021 high.
UAE quits OPEC amid Gulf tensions, adding to oil supply uncertainty.
China factory activity tops expectations but growth slows; euro zone inflation jumps to 3%.
AI spending by hyperscalers set to reach $725B in 2026; LinkedIn AI hiring agents on track for $450M revenue.
Jobless claims fall to 189,000, lowest in over 50 years, signaling tight labor market.
Soybeans and bean oil rally to contract highs; corn gains modestly.
Ceres Power shares surge nearly 1,000% on fuel cell rally; energy stocks favored by top investors.

sectors.trend: Strong rally driven by Iran war, blockade, and OPEC exit by UAE; oil near $120, gas at $4.30, aluminum gains.
sectors.implications: Energy stocks and commodities are surging; inflation pressures mount; transportation and consumer discretionary sectors face headwinds.

sectors.trend: Mixed: Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet beat earnings but AI spending surge ($725B capex) pressures margins; Meta falls despite strong earnings; AMD upgraded on GPU demand; Intel up 115% in April.
sectors.implications: AI remains dominant theme but capex intensity raises free cash flow concerns; hyperscaler spending supports chip and cloud stocks but may lead to volatility.

sectors.trend: Soybeans rally to contract highs on bean oil strength; corn gains modestly; India heat wave stresses grid and crops.
sectors.implications: Agricultural commodities are rising, benefiting producers but adding to food inflation; India's energy crunch may disrupt supply chains.

sectors.trend: Fed holds rates steady; ECB signals possible June hike; euro zone inflation at 3%; jobless claims at 50-year low.
sectors.implications: Tight labor market and sticky inflation keep central banks hawkish; higher-for-longer rates support bank margins but pressure growth stocks.

sectors.trend: Aluminum gains on supply hole and blockade; gold and silver rise on dollar weakness; rare earths in focus.
sectors.implications: Commodity supercycle continues; geopolitical risks boost precious metals; supply constraints support prices.

sectors.trend: Gas prices at 4-year high weigh on consumer spending; college costs rise; home sales highest since 2021.
sectors.implications: Energy costs are a drag on disposable income; housing market shows resilience but affordability remains challenged.

sectors.trend: Aurobindo Pharma gets Health Canada nod for biosimilar; DeFi and blockchain expanding into wealth management.
sectors.implications: Biosimilars offer growth in pharma; healthcare remains defensive amid macro uncertainty.

sectors.trend: SPX Technologies raises guidance despite tariff headwinds; Union Pacific highlighted as Harvard stock pick.
sectors.implications: Tariffs and supply chain disruptions are manageable for some industrials; infrastructure spending supports long-term demand.

sectors.trend: Home sales in March highest since 2021; prices stable; insurance costs rise for water damage.
sectors.implications: Housing market remains strong but affordability and insurance costs are headwinds.

key_themes: Iran war and energy crisis: Oil near $120, gas at $4.30, UAE leaves OPEC, blockade persists.
AI spending boom: Hyperscaler capex to reach $725B in 2026; Big Tech earnings beat but capex concerns rise.
Central bank divergence: Fed holds, ECB hawkish, Japan intervenes in yen.
Inflation and labor market: Jobless claims at 50-year low, euro zone inflation at 3%, Fed holds rates.
Geopolitical risks: Taiwan chokepoint, Iran blockade, China rare earths, US-China trade tensions.
Commodity rally: Soybeans, corn, aluminum, gold, silver all gaining.
Yen intervention and energy correlation: Yen weakens as oil rises, intervention fails to hold.
Regulatory and political pressure: FCC reviews ABC licenses, bill to ban Chinese vehicles, Disney license renewals.
DeFi and blockchain expansion: Meta pays creators in stablecoins, Blockchain.com launches wealth program.
Energy transition: Fuel cell rally (Ceres Power), renewable energy stocks gain.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Iran war and energy supply disruption
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase allocation to energy stocks and commodities; hedge with oil futures or ETFs; reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and transport.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Stagflation from high energy prices and sticky inflation
risk_assessment.mitigation: Favor value stocks, commodities, and TIPS; reduce duration in bonds; hold cash for opportunities.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI capex bubble and Big Tech margin compression
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify within tech; focus on companies with strong free cash flow; consider semiconductor and cloud infrastructure plays.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Geopolitical escalation in Taiwan and Middle East
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to defense stocks, gold, and energy; reduce exposure to semiconductor supply chain stocks.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Yen intervention failure and currency volatility
risk_assessment.mitigation: Hedge yen exposure; invest in Japanese trading houses benefiting from energy prices; avoid Japanese government bonds.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory and political risks (FCC, Chinese vehicles, Disney)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Monitor regulatory developments; avoid media stocks with license risks; consider defense and domestic manufacturing.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: China economic slowdown and trade tensions
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to China-sensitive sectors; favor domestic-oriented stocks; monitor rare earth and commodity prices.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Euro zone stagflation and ECB tightening
risk_assessment.mitigation: Underweight European equities; favor US and emerging markets; hedge EUR exposure.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase allocation to energy stocks and commodities
opportunities.rationale: Iran war and OPEC exit by UAE are driving oil toward $120; energy stocks offer strong earnings momentum and dividends.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy AI infrastructure and semiconductor stocks on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: Hyperscaler capex of $725B in 2026 supports long-term demand for chips and cloud; AMD upgrade and Intel rally signal strength.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in agricultural commodities and related ETFs
opportunities.rationale: Soybeans and corn rallying on supply concerns; India heat wave adds to food inflation; bean oil at contract highs.

opportunities.recommendation: Add gold and silver for geopolitical hedge
opportunities.rationale: Precious metals rising on dollar weakness and Iran tensions; central bank buying supports prices.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider Japanese trading houses for energy exposure
opportunities.rationale: Energy prices underpin strong outlook for trading houses; yen intervention may stabilize currency.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy high-yield savings and CDs for cash allocation
opportunities.rationale: Rates up to 4.15% APY on CDs and 4.1% on savings provide safe yield amid volatility.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and transport stocks
defensive_moves.rationale: Gas prices at $4.30 and oil near $120 will squeeze consumer spending and raise operating costs.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against yen depreciation and currency volatility
defensive_moves.rationale: Yen intervention failing as energy prices rise; correlation with oil at 2021 high.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Underweight European equities due to stagflation risks
defensive_moves.rationale: Euro zone inflation at 3%, growth stalling, ECB likely to hike; FTSE 100 falling.
Fed hold and ECB hawkishness keep rates higher for longer; inflation risks persist.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid media stocks with regulatory risks
defensive_moves.rationale: FCC reviewing ABC licenses and Disney renewals; political pressure on broadcasters.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets are likely to remain volatile with a bullish tilt in energy and commodities, while tech faces headwinds from AI capex concerns and rising rates. The Iran war and oil prices will dominate sentiment. The Fed's hold and tight labor market support a 'higher for longer' rate environment. Yen intervention may provide temporary relief but energy prices will counter. S&P 500 futures are falling after mixed Big Tech earnings, suggesting near-term weakness.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: The energy crisis could persist if Iran blockade continues, pushing oil above $120 and fueling global inflation. AI spending boom will drive long-term growth in tech, but margin compression and regulatory risks may cap upside. Central banks will remain hawkish, with ECB hiking and Fed on hold. Geopolitical risks (Taiwan, Middle East) will keep safe-haven assets in demand. Commodities and value stocks are favored over growth. The yen may weaken further if energy prices stay high.
key_catalysts: Iran war developments: blockade, nuclear deal, or escalation.
OPEC+ response to UAE exit and supply disruptions.
Fed and ECB policy decisions in June.
Big Tech earnings and AI capex guidance.
US jobless claims and inflation data.
Yen intervention effectiveness and energy price correlation.
China economic stimulus and trade talks.
Taiwan geopolitical tensions and semiconductor supply chain.
US election year dynamics and regulatory actions.
watch_list: Oil price (WTI, Brent) and gas prices at the pump.
US 10-year Treasury yield and yield curve.
Iran war headlines and blockade updates.
US jobless claims and CPI reports.
